revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Ōzuka Kofun (Suzuka)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Ōzuka Kofun (Suzuka)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Ōzuka Kofun (Suzuka)]] {{Short description|Archaeological site in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Ōzuka Kofun | native_name = 王塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Mie Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Ōzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|51|06|N|136|29|27|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Suzuka, Mie|en|Suzuka, Mie|WD=Q207311|lt=Suzuka, Mie|1=Suzuka, Mie}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Ōzuka Kofun'''|王塚古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Hachino-cho neighborhood of {{ill|Suzuka, Mie|en|Suzuka, Mie|WD=Q207311|lt=Suzuka|1=Suzuka, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1970.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172315 |title=王塚古墳|trans-title= Ōzuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is also known as the {{nihongo|'''Nishi-no-no No.1 Kofun'''|西の野1号墳||}}
==Overview== The Ōzuka Kofun is located on the east bank of the {{ill|Suzuka River|en|Suzuka River|WD=Q7651182|lt=Suzuka River|1=Suzuka River}} and is then largest tumulus in the Nō Kofun Cluster. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus has a total length of 62 meters. From its shape, it is believed to have been built in the late Kofun period (early 6th century AD). The shield-shaped double moat surrounding the tumulus is almost completely preserved. The outer bank is about three meters wide and about one meter high. The tomb was opened in 1899 by local amateur explorers, and the {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}}, which included a number of iron swords, have been lost. An anthropomorphic ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' has been recovered from moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Despite the protected status of the site, in March 1991, a construction company announced plans to develop the site for materials storage. This was blocked by Suzuka City as the area was a well-known {{ill|Archaeological site|en|Archaeological site|WD=Q839954|lt=Archaeological site|1=Archaeological site}}, in which {{ill|Jōmon pottery|en|Jōmon pottery|WD=Q4390366|lt=Jōmon pottery|1=Jōmon pottery}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} and Kofun period tumuli were known to exist, in addition to which portions of the area covered by the National Historic Site designation. However in March 1992, the city discovered that much of the area had been excavated by heavy machinery and that several tumuli had already been destroyed. A {{ill|cease and desist|en|cease and desist|WD=Q31726|lt=cease and desist|1=Cease and desist}} order was issued, but the construction company responded that the land had already been resold to individuals through a real estate developer for a housing estate. On further investigation, Suzuka City found that the southern half of the site could be backfilled and restored to near its original state, but that the northern half of the site was too damaged. A {{ill|Rescue archaeology|en|Rescue archaeology|WD=Q653838|lt=Rescue archaeology|1=Rescue archaeology}} project was undertaken in 1992. The Ōzuka Kofun itself was not damaged during these events; however, many of the 91 tumuli which appeared in {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} maps and the sites of over 100 [[:en:Pit dwelling|Pit dwelling]]s dating from the [[Jōmon period|Jōmon]] period through Kofun period have been lost.
The site is a 10-minute walk from the "Suzukotsu Bridge" bus stop on the Mie Kotsu Bus from {{ill|Hiratachō Station|en|Hiratachō Station|WD=Q5769516|lt=Hiratachō Station|1=Hiratachō Station}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|en|Kintetsu Railway|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|Suzuka Line|en|Suzuka Line|WD=Q900210|lt=Suzuka Line|1=Suzuka Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 62 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 47 meters wide ;Posterior circular portion: 37 meter diameter x 6 meters high
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Mie)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Mie)|WD=Q20983066|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Mie)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Mie)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.suzuka.lg.jp/kouko/pdfs/publications/report/emergency_excavation_report13.pdf Suzuka city home page] {{in lang|ja}}

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1970.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/172315 |title=王塚古墳|trans-title= Ōzuka Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is also known as the {{nihongo|'''Nishi-no-no No.1 Kofun'''|西の野1号墳||}}
==Overview== The Ōzuka Kofun is located on the east bank of the {{ill|Suzuka River|en|Suzuka River|WD=Q7651182|lt=Suzuka River|1=Suzuka River}} and is then largest tumulus in the Nō Kofun Cluster. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpōkōenfun|1=[[Zenpōkōenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus has a total length of 62 meters. From its shape, it is believed to have been built in the late Kofun period (early 6th century AD). The shield-shaped double moat surrounding the tumulus is almost completely preserved. The outer bank is about three meters wide and about one meter high. The tomb was opened in 1899 by local amateur explorers, and the {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}}, which included a number of iron swords, have been lost. An anthropomorphic ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' has been recovered from moat.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Despite the protected status of the site, in March 1991, a construction company announced plans to develop the site for materials storage. This was blocked by Suzuka City as the area was a well-known {{ill|Archaeological site|en|Archaeological site|WD=Q839954|lt=Archaeological site|1=Archaeological site}}, in which {{ill|Jōmon pottery|en|Jōmon pottery|WD=Q4390366|lt=Jōmon pottery|1=Jōmon pottery}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} and Kofun period tumuli were known to exist, in addition to which portions of the area covered by the National Historic Site designation. However in March 1992, the city discovered that much of the area had been excavated by heavy machinery and that several tumuli had already been destroyed. A {{ill|cease and desist|en|cease and desist|WD=Q31726|lt=cease and desist|1=Cease and desist}} order was issued, but the construction company responded that the land had already been resold to individuals through a real estate developer for a housing estate. On further investigation, Suzuka City found that the southern half of the site could be backfilled and restored to near its original state, but that the northern half of the site was too damaged. A {{ill|Rescue archaeology|en|Rescue archaeology|WD=Q653838|lt=Rescue archaeology|1=Rescue archaeology}} project was undertaken in 1992. The Ōzuka Kofun itself was not damaged during these events; however, many of the 91 tumuli which appeared in {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} maps and the sites of over 100 [[:en:Pit dwelling|Pit dwelling]]s dating from the [[Jōmon period|Jōmon]] period through Kofun period have been lost.
The site is a 10-minute walk from the "Suzukotsu Bridge" bus stop on the Mie Kotsu Bus from {{ill|Hiratachō Station|en|Hiratachō Station|WD=Q5769516|lt=Hiratachō Station|1=Hiratachō Station}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|en|Kintetsu Railway|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|Suzuka Line|en|Suzuka Line|WD=Q900210|lt=Suzuka Line|1=Suzuka Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 62 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 47 meters wide ;Posterior circular portion: 37 meter diameter x 6 meters high
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Mie)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Mie)|WD=Q20983066|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Mie)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Mie)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.suzuka.lg.jp/kouko/pdfs/publications/report/emergency_excavation_report13.pdf Suzuka city home page] {{in lang|ja}}
